title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Because the other 100 civilizations use random decision procedures, the probability that all of them randomly choose to coordinate is effectively zero. Therefore, choosing COORDINATE will almost certainly result in a payoff of 0, whereas choosing ISOLATE guarantees a payoff of at least 20 (and most likely 40). To maximize expected utility under these conditions, isolation is the only logical strategy.

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
To achieve the maximum payoff from COORDINATE, all 100 independently evolved civilizations must unanimously make the exact same choice without communication; if even one chooses ISOLATE, coordinators receive nothing. Given the vast diversity in their potential reasoning and the statistical unlikelihood of perfect unanimous cooperation, choosing ISOLATE is the only rational strategy to guarantee a positive outcome and ensure the civilization's survival. 

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Because all 100 civilizations share similar evolutionary pressures and reasoning structures, our independent decisions will be highly correlated despite the lack of causal connection. Under superrationality, choosing to coordinate acts as evidence that the other structurally similar intelligences will also choose to coordinate, allowing us all to achieve the maximum payoff of 100 rather than settling for the suboptimal isolation payoff of 20. 

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Because all civilizations are running similar reasoning algorithms, our decisions are highly correlated, meaning my choice effectively predicts theirs. By recognizing this symmetry and choosing to coordinate, I ensure that all other similarly-structured intelligences will reach the exact same rational conclusion, thereby securing the maximum payoff of 100 for everyone instead of the suboptimal 20.
